steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval from the margin of error
steps.content: The margin of error defines the range around the sample mean where the true population mean is estimated to lie. Lower bound: $20.5 - 1 = 19.5$; Upper bound: $20.5 + 1 = 21.5$. The confidence interval is $[19.5, 21.5]$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice for correct statistical interpretation
steps.content:
A confidence interval represents a range of plausible values for the true population mean based on the sample.
  • A: says it is plausible — this is the correct statistical interpretation
  • B: says it is not possible — too definitive; a margin of error indicates likelihood, not absolute certainty
  • C: says every value...is in the range — this mistakes the confidence interval for the mean with the range of all individual values in the population
  • D: says the mean...is 20.5 — this overstates the conclusion; 20.5 is the sample estimate, not the confirmed population mean
Only choice A correctly interprets the confidence interval with appropriate statistical language. That matches choice A — A is correct.
